feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
feed.subtitle: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Create supplier invoice app and import supplier invoice app does not flow to DRC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We noticed that when we use "Create Supplier Invoice" app and "Import Supplier Invoice" app to create the Finance invoices, it doesn't flow to SAP DRC.

However, it is working fine if using "Create Incoming Invoices" app. For MM invoices from "Create Supplier Invoice -Advanced" app, there is no issue as well. It is able to flow to SAP DRC.

Anyone has any thoughts?

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP Document Reporting and Compliance Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I would like to know what are the differences of using SAP Document compliance and Reporting Cloud Edition compared to the scope id 5XU (SAP Document compliance and Reporting)? If we are using SAP Document Compliance and Cloud edition, then do the edocuments created appear in the edocument cockpit inside S4 or is it available only in BTP cockpit?

entry.title: SAP AFO for Mac users
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

SAP AFO(Analysis for Office) is not available for Mac users as it is delivered via Excel add-in .EXE file.

I have looked for many forums but could not find any solution. If you are also looking to enable AFO for Mac Users please do consider voting for this customer influence improvement request. 
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/372281/?section=sectionDetail

If there is any workaround on this, please do share your thoughts.

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP DRC Configuration
entry.content:
Hello Community,

Looking for SAP DRC Configuration and process.

Anyone can brief it like functional SD and FI configuration and BTP configuration.

Means a lot

I have analyzed few things related to this sharing with you all but still configuration steps and BTO configuration steps required.

Bullet Points 

SAP DRC Definition And Usage

SAP DRC High Level Architecture 

Supported Compliance Tasks by Country or Region

KSA Supported Scenarios

SAP DRC Cloud Edition Activation Steps

Steps 

SAP DRC Cloud Edition is hosted on SAP BTP and connect backend ERP (S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA On-Prem) to local tax/government authorities .

Pre-requisites

  1. BTP Global Account CPEA or Pay-as-you-go
  2. Subaccount with Entitlement to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition

Activation Steps :

you can run BTP Booster for automatic setup or use below Manual Setup .

1. Entitle the Service in BTP Cockpit

  • Go to your BTP Global Account
  • Navigate to the Subaccount you want to use
  • Open Entitlements Configure Entitlements
  • Add Service Plan: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
  • Select default or the appropriate service plan .

2. Create Service Instance and Service Key

  • Go to Instances and Subscriptions in your BTP Subaccount

 

Click Create → Service Instance

Choose: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition

Choose the plan (default) , Set a name

  • After creation, Create Service Key

You’ll use the key (credentials, endpoint) to connect your ERP .

3. Setup Trust & Connectivity

  • If ERP is in S/4HANA Public Cloud: no middleware needed
  • For On-Premise or Private Cloud, set up:

 

-SAP Cloud Connector (to connect backend)

-Trust relationship with SAP Identity Authentication (IAS) if needed

-SAP Integration Suite if required by the scenario

4. Configure Communication Arrangement in ERP

In S/4HANA Cloud:

Use SSCUI or CBC to activate relevant scope items (e.g., 5XU, 5XN, etc.)

Create Communication Arrangements to connect ERP to DRC Cloud via APIs

In S/4HANA On-Premise:

Use SOAMANAGER or manually set up RFC destinations / HTTP connections

-Activate required BC Sets and Business Functions .

Looking forward to hearing you step by step configuration and BTP setup.

Thanks,

Ashish

entry.title: SCM Tech Writers?
entry.content:
Ok, ok... just for the wisenheimer 😉.... 

(SCM) Supply Chain Management, (LOB) Line of Business, What is a BISO (Business Information Security Officer) and what exactly do they do?

Note: I would especially be interested in folks who utilize Git and GitHub for versioning, content management.

Hello,

The SCM BISO group is new to SCM and as the contractor working with the team, I would like to know if there are any other documentation specialist/technical writers in LOB.

I would like to connect and also see resources and advice they might share with a newbie!

Thanks!

Julie

entry.title: Poland KSeF eDocument Cockpit - "The data object could not be created: The type does not exist"
entry.content:
We are implementing Poland KSeF e-invoicing on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, following the official SAP documentation  and note 3708755. Authentication is via KSeF certificate.

The setup works perfectly in our test system. In production, however, every attempt to submit or display the XML of a Poland KSeF eInvoice in the eDocument Cockpit fails with:

"The data object could not be created: The type  does not exist. Message no. EDOCUMENT000"

Notice the double space between the words "type" and "does".

We have deleted and recreated all settings multiple times, always with identical results. We are confident the configuration is correct — the test system proves it.

Our experience with SAP Support so far has been frustrating. We first raised this case on April 9th. After nearly a month, we still have no resolution, no committed timeline, and no feedback from any technical team, with the case being bounced from one department to another. When we pushed for an update, we were informally told that the root cause is likely already known internally — yet no fix has been delivered, and no transparent communication has been proactively shared with us. For a production blocker with direct legal and financial consequences — KSeF compliance is now mandatory in Poland — this is simply not acceptable.

We would like to know:

  • Is anyone else experiencing this exact error in production while the test system works fine?
  • Has anyone found a workaround or received a fix from SAP?
  • Are there any clues about which specific component or setting causes the test/production discrepancy?

Any input is deeply appreciated

entry.title: Headers/Additional Properties for SAP Business Network(Buyer) Destination in SAP DRC
entry.content:
Hello All,

I wanted to know what all headers/Additional Properties we need to configure in the destination setting for SAP Business Network(Buyer) in SAP DRC. 
Currently I am setting below Headers and getting error http 500( Error occurred during the step, detailed information: The Cloud Edition received the following message when sending information that comes from the external communication party to your business system with destination SAPARIBA: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'value') The process instance ID is 4b121e20-ab47-XXXXXXXX, HTTP status code is 500) :

 
KeyValue
drccSystemTypeOther
scope

ariba/api

URL.headers.X-ARIBA-NETWORK-IDAN0XXXXXXXXXX-T
URL.headers.apiKey
<api Key>

Note : In my case we are trying to pick the invoice from KSEF and post it in SAP Business Network(Buyer) using SAP DRC.

entry.title: DRC EDOC FI Document with more than 999-line items
entry.content:
How DRC Edocument manage FI Document with more than 999 line items?
we have a scenario where RV, DR, DM documents are having more than 999 line items. How SAP DRC system creates e-documents for this scenario.?

entry.title: Using SAP DRC with residual payments
entry.content:
Hello experts -

We would like to implement SAP DRC for our Mexico business unit.  However, today we use residual payment methodology in SAP, not partial payments.  We have been told that you must use partial payments in order to be able to use SAP DRC.  This seems strange to us, and residual payments are standard SAP functionality. Also, changing from residual to partial payment would be a major change for us, and we do not want to make this change.  Is it possible to use SAP DRC if you use residual payments?  Does it require complex customization in order to be able to do so?

entry.title: Creating Statutory Reports for Non-Supported Countries in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently working on implementing statutory and legal reporting requirements for a country that is not officially supported by SAP standard localization content in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Our objective is to:

  • Create country-specific statutory reports
  • Adapt report layouts to local legal formats
  • Configure electronic or manual declarations
  • Use available SAP tools such as:
    • Define Statutory Reports
    • Compliance / DRC applications
    • Custom CDS Views
    • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

We would like to understand the recommended approach and best practices for Public Cloud environments.

Main questions:

  1. What is the standard approach to create statutory reports for non-supported countries in SAP Public Cloud?
  2. Which applications or SSCUIs should be used?
  3. Is it possible to fully customize report queries, mappings, and layouts?
  4. Can custom CDS Views be integrated directly into statutory reporting frameworks?
  5. What are the limitations in Public Cloud compared to Private Cloud / On-Premise?
  6. Has anyone implemented local tax or financial declarations for unsupported countries (example: Morocco)?
  7. Is SAP Document and Reporting Compliance extensible enough for fully custom legal reports?

Any guidance, architecture examples, implementation steps, or lessons learned would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: France B2B e-invoicing 2026 - correct eDocument process code in DRC (FR_B2B vs FRSINV)?
entry.content:
Configuring SAP S/4HANA for the French B2B e-invoicing mandate (PA/Y-model, September 2026). Two specific questions:

1. Process code: Is FR_B2B the correct eDocument process code for outbound B2B invoices under the France PA mandate, or is FRSINV still current? We understand FRSINV may be legacy Chorus Pro / pilot. Is FR_B2B_CRN the correct type for credit notes (avoir)?

2. SAP as PA: SAP was announced as a certified Plateforme Agréée in February 2026. Does this mean customers using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance cloud edition (BTP) can transmit without contracting a separate third-party PDP? Is this limited to DRC Cloud Edition only?

Context: deploying for a customer using SAP DRC. Would be interested to hear from practitioners on both On-Premise and DRC Cloud Edition.

Any practitioner who has gone live or is in active testing for France B2B PA would be extremely helpful.

entry.title: Integrating SII for Spain with SAP DRC
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We are currently submitting VAT-relevant information to the Spanish tax authorities using SII, and we would like to integrate this process with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

We performed all the integration steps according to the SAP Help Portal and tested by submitting an invoice to the test environment of the tax authorities, but got the following error: You need to define the result for process ESSIIBUN step DRCC_RESP first (EDOCPROCSETFLAG). (screenshot below)

We think that it means that we have to configure incoming messages from external systems, but we are not sure how to configure this. Does anyone know why this error occurs and how to resolve it?

Thank you!

entry.title: DRC - Error: value not found while trying a new Statutory Reporting Run
entry.content:
Hi every one, 

I need your help to solve this error: While executing a new run using the Run Statutory Reporting App I am encountering an error message: Value not found COMPANYCODE.

In our TDD, we only have the company code DO10, which is where I have the data.

Could you please guide me on how to resolve this error?

I am attaching screenshots of the error and the company code assignment in the configuration.

Johan Campusano

entry.title: UK Postponed Import VAT Accounting in SAP
entry.content:
Dear Community, 

I am looking for guidance from anyone with UK VAT / HMRC experience in SAP, specifically regarding the correct accounting treatment for monthly Postponed Import VAT (PIVA).

Background:
Each month our customer receive a postponed import VAT statement from HMRC. In the previous system, they could simply upload the statement into the VAT return module and manually enter the figures. The system automatically created the necessary contra postings between input VAT and output VAT.

In SAP, however, this process does not appear to be straightforward.

What we are trying to achieve:
We need to post a VAT-only document that correctly updates both:

  • Input VAT G/L account
  • Output VAT G/L account

without impacting supplier balances or net expense values.

What we have researched so far:
One suggested approach was to create a VAT-only supplier invoice using a VF tax code (or similar configuration involving tax-only postings / reverse charge logic).

We tested this in our SAP test system, but we are encountering configuration-related errors when attempting multiple assignment lines for the tax code. It appears SAP may require additional tax code configuration to allow this process.

Questions:

  1. How are UK companies typically handling postponed import VAT accounting in SAP?
  2. Is there a standard SAP process for HMRC PIVA postings?

Thank you very much in advance for any guidance.

Best regards

Vanessa

#SAP #SAPFI #UKVAT #PostponedImportVAT #S4HANA #DRC

entry.title: E-Invoicing for self billing in France E-Invoice
entry.content: We are implementing SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) for France E‑Invoicing. As part of the mandate, we are required to use self‑invoicing; however, SAP currently does not provide a standard solution for this scenario. According to French compliance requirements, self‑invoices must be generated by us through MRRL and subsequently transmitted to the approved Plateformes de Dématérialisation Partenaires (PAs). We kindly request your guidance and a proposed solution to address this gap, as this is critical for ensuring compliance with the French e‑invoicing law. Thank you in advance for your support and prompt attention to this matter.

entry.title: SAP DRC strategy: ViDA readiness and global e-invoicing and e-reporting rollout
entry.content:
Our company runs primarily on US GAAP with limited local statutory ledger setup (no country version available) in many countries. Does that create gaps for DRC?

For example, missing local tax codes, document types or master data that the country reports require?  

How does a DRC implementation actually unfold in a country like France, E2E?

entry.title: Why is report DE_AUDIT_EUBP (DRC) only valid from 1 January 2024?
entry.content:
Hi,

my customer needs to provide the legal report DE_AUDIT_EUBP for the fiscal year 2022. But when trying to set up the actitivites, I can only choose "Valid From" 2024 and it is not possible to change. 

Is there any way to use the report for earlier fiscal years?  If not, what's the alternative?

Thank you and best regards

Rado

entry.title: Question on GTI License Requirements for China Golden Tax Integration Scenarios
entry.content:
Hi SAP colleagues,
I would like to get your advice on the license requirements related to SAP Golden Tax Interface (GTI) in China localization.
We are currently evaluating different integration approaches between SAP and the Chinese Golden Tax System, and I would like to confirm whether my understanding of the licensing implications is correct.
■ Background
We assume that SAP SD billing data is transferred to the Golden Tax Interface (GTI), where data validation, splitting, and consolidation are performed before invoice issuance.
■ Integration Scenarios
1. Manual Integration
SAP outputs billing data in the required format of the Golden Tax System.
The data is then manually processed and uploaded into the Golden Tax System to issue invoices.
2. Integration via Third-Party System
SAP sends data in the format required by a third-party vendor, and the third-party system handles the integration with the Golden Tax System.
3. Direct Integration
SAP directly sends data to the Golden Tax System via APIs/interfaces for invoice issuance.
■ Questions
1. For Scenario 1 (manual integration): If we use standard GTI functions in SAP (such as data extraction, validation, splitting, or consolidation), would a GTI-related license still be required?
2. For Scenarios 2 and 3: Is it correct to understand that a GTI (or related) license is required whenever SAP is involved in the integration process?
3. In an S/4HANA environment:
When using solutions such as
- SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC)
- China Tax e-Invoice Management how do these relate to GTI licensing?
Are additional licenses required?
Any insights or experience sharing would be greatly appreciated.
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP DRC - Electronic Invoicing for Italy : ItalyReceiveNotification without SNI
entry.content:
Hello 

I am currently implementing SAP DRC - Electronic Invoicing for Italy.
We successfully managed to send documents to SdI, but unfortunately we are facing an issue with the ItalyReceiveNotification process. When SdI calls this service, they receive an HTTP 500 error. This could be related to the fact that SdI does not support SNI.

I found a forum discussion that refers to this issue: https://forum.italia.it/t/problema-test-interoperabilita/45870/7 
But I have not been able to find a concrete solution. I would like to understand whether SAP can resolve this issue on its side, or whether it must be addressed by our network team, for example by implementing a reverse proxy.

entry.title: E-Reporting Vs E-Invoicing for External Billing Documents
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to establish a link between E-Invoicing versus E-Reporting for Invoices which are coming to SAP DRC from non-SAP system. Non-SAP systems feeds the invoice data to SAP DRC in UBL 2.1 format and then SAP DRC creates its e-Document .

In the BP we maintain country key=FR and FR2 (SIREN) with taxstatus=PRA hence making the customer as established entity in France. so it qualifies for the Domestic B2B transaction and hence the E-Invoicing.

In some customers we maintain country key=FR and FR2 (SIREN) with taxstatus=NOR hence making the customer as Non-established entity in France. so it qualifies for the Domestic B2C transaction and hence E-Reporting.

For SAP native invoices i-e Billing document generated via SD or FI postings, above BP logic is working fine and it is routing the invoice data correctly to E-Invoicing or E-Reporting folder.

For the invoices coming from non-SAP system as UBL invoice, necessary business transformation is happening in the middleware and then it is replicated to SAP DRC and SAP DRC is creating e-Invoice for every case disregarding the FR2 TAXSTATUS=PRA OR NOR.  I am not sure why SAP standard logic does not work  for these invoices coming from external systems. Has anyone encountered this issue ? Will SAP standard logic not work for non-SAP invoices coming in UBL 2.1 format and we will have to do this transformation in middleware ?

Any experiences will be appreciated.

Thanks

Atif
